name: Puntal de la Paridera and Pico Almenara
name_original: Puntal de la Paridera y Pico Almenara
region_intro: Hiking around Paterna Del Madera offers diverse natural landscapes within the Sierra del Agua and Sierra del Segura. The region features mountainous terrain with peaks like Almenara (1,796 m) and La Sarga (1,769 m), providing extensive views. River valleys, including the Alto Guadalquivir, and waterfalls such as Batán Waterfall and El Chorreaero, contribute to the varied scenery. Natural parks like Parque Natural Los Calares del Mundo y La Sima are also accessible, showcasing unique geological formations. Best hiking trails around Paterna Del Madera The most popular hiking route is Puntal de la Paridera and Pico Almenara, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) trail that takes about 5 hours to complete, offering views from Almenara Peak. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hike along the Madera River from Batán del Puerto, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This route follows the Madera River, passing through scenic spots where rivers converge. Local hikers also love the Almenara Peak – Hiking markers loop from Cortijo de Tortas, a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) trail leading through the high peaks of the region, often completed in about 5 hours. Hiking around Paterna Del Madera is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and waterfalls. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Paterna Del Madera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Paterna Del Madera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Paterna del Madera?
A: Paterna del Madera offers a diverse network of trails suitable for various abilities. While there are challenging routes, the majority of the 19 available trails are rated as moderate. There is also at least one easy trail, making the region accessible for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed walk.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Paterna del Madera?
A: Yes, Paterna del Madera has options for easier hikes. While many trails are moderate, there is at least one easy route available. These trails typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them ideal for families or those new to hiking. The extensive network ensures you can find a path that matches your comfort level.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Paterna del Madera?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its impressive waterfalls. You can visit the 10-meter high &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5318691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, surrounded by pine and holm oak forests. Another notable waterfall is El Chorreaero, fed by the Juanfría spring, which sometimes freezes in winter to create a spectacular ice formation. Many trails in the area will lead you near these beautiful natural features.

Q: Which trails offer the best panoramic views in Paterna del Madera?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that ascend to the higher peaks. &lt;strong&gt;Almenara Peak&lt;/strong&gt;, standing at 1,796 meters, is a significant highlight, offering extensive vistas of the surrounding sierras and plains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368919891/puntal-de-la-paridera-and-pico-almenara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de la Paridera and Pico Almenara&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent choice for experiencing these views.

Q: Are there any unique natural landmarks or ancient trees to see while hiking?
A: Yes, Paterna del Madera is rich in unique natural landmarks. You can discover the ancient &lt;strong&gt;Tejo Viejo&lt;/strong&gt;, a rare yew tree over a thousand years old, or the spectacularly sized &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5227975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pino Gordo del Toril&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;. The region also features crystal-clear springs like Fuente Lisa, Fuente de los Bandoleros, and Fuente de los Muchachos, which are often found along hiking paths.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see on hikes around Paterna del Madera?
A: Hiking around Paterna del Madera offers incredibly diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter mountainous terrain with high peaks, lush river valleys like the Alto Guadalquivir, and impressive waterfalls. The area is characterized by extensive pine and holm oak forests, unique karst geological formations, and scenic spots where rivers converge, such as Batán del Puerto. The nearby Parque Natural Los Calares del Mundo y La Sima also showcases spectacular rock formations.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Paterna del Madera?
A: The region is suitable for hiking across multiple seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winters can bring colder temperatures, and some higher elevation areas might experience snow or ice, particularly around waterfalls like El Chorreaero.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Paterna del Madera?
A: Yes, Paterna del Madera offers several excellent circular hiking routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Peak – Hiking markers loop from Cortijo de Tortas&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that takes you through the region's high peaks. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19410236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Madera – Batán del Puerto loop from Paterna del Madera&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the river and offers scenic views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Paterna del Madera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, including the stunning mountain views from peaks like Almenara, the serene river valleys, and the impressive waterfalls. The well-maintained and extensive network of trails, catering to various difficulty levels, is also a common highlight.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, there are several points of interest. You can explore the charming settlement of &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6006591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bogarra&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; or the historic &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;. The region is also close to the Parque Natural Los Calares del Mundo y La Sima and El Nacimiento Del Rio Mundo, offering further exploration opportunities.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or offer waterside views?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by its river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1076463765/hike-along-the-madera-river-from-batan-del-puerto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike along the Madera River from Batán del Puerto&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, following the Madera River and passing through picturesque spots where rivers like the Viñazos, Endrinales, and Hoyas converge. These routes provide refreshing waterside views and a different perspective of the landscape.
tips: Beautiful views of Puntal de la Paridera.

This fountain is located on a popular hiking route that leads to the top of Pico Almenara, the highest point in the mountain range.

The fountain is an ideal place to rest and recharge your batteries before continuing the climb. The route that passes by the fountain offers spectacular views and is known for its natural beauty.

Almenara Peak is a 1796 meter high mountain in the Alcaraz mountain range. It is important for its visibility from many kilometers and for being the highest point of this mountain range.

It is not a mountain that is difficult to access on any of its sides, but the normal ascent route is on its south face from the source of Pino de los Muchachos.

waypoints.name: Fuente del Pino de los Muchachos
